explanation: The requirement is that the self-test shall confirm (i.e., pass) that the Fl2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit is connected in the right direction and is calibrated. The closest atomic proposition available is 'testFl2Pass', which directly indicates a successful self-test for the Fl2 flow meter. Thus, the system in SelfTest Mode (represented by SelfTestMode) must ensure that testFl2Pass holds (i.e., is true) (i.e., the test passes for the Fl2 flow meter). The best-matching temporal logic is 'Globally, if in SelfTest mode, then the Fl2 flow meter test must pass.' This is captured by G (SelfTestMode -> testFl2Pass). This guarantees that whenever the system is in self-test mode, the Fl2 flow meter test pass confirmation is required, capturing the natural language requirement accurately.

The requirement is that during the self-test procedure, the system must confirm that the Fl2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit is connected correctly and is calibrated. We map this as: whenever the self-test is active (indicated by 'SelfTestMode'), it must always eventually result in a successful Fl2 flow meter test ('testFl2Pass'). This ensures both connection direction and calibration are confirmed as part of self-test. We use 'G (SelfTestMode -> (F[0,N_DURATION] testFl2Pass))' with a chosen duration N_DURATION=3 to express that, throughout self-test mode, the Fl2 flow meter must be confirmed (pass) within the allowed steps or time.

The requirement specifies that during the self-test procedure, the system must confirm the FI2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit is correctly connected and calibrated. As per the provided atomic propositions, 'testFl2Pass' represents a successful FI2 flow meter test. To faithfully capture the requirement, the LTL property must state that whenever the system is in SelfTestMode, eventually the FI2 flow meter test must pass (i.e., testFl2Pass becomes true). Thus, we use the template 'G (bool_exp2 -> (F bool_exp3))', meaning whenever the self-test procedure is active (SelfTestMode), eventually the FI2 flow meter test passes (testFl2Pass). This LTL formula captures the intent that the self-test must confirm the FI2 flow meter is connected and calibrated before self-test completion.

The requirement is that the self-test procedure must confirm that the Fl2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit is connected correctly and is calibrated. The atomic proposition 'testFl2Pass' represents the passing of the Fl2 flow meter test, which implies its confirmation of connection and calibration. To ensure that this confirmation is always achieved whenever a self-test is performed, we specify that in all executions of the self-test procedure, the property 'testFl2Pass' must eventually hold (i.e., the self-test process leads to a confirmed, correctly connected, calibrated Fl2 flow meter). This is captured by LTL template 19: 'G (bool_exp2 -> (F bool_exp3))', where 'bool_exp2' is 'SelfTestMode' (representing the self-test procedure is active) and 'bool_exp3' is 'testFl2Pass' (the needed confirmation). N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The requirement is that during the self-test procedure, the system must verify (confirm) that the FI2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit is both connected in the correct direction and calibrated. Since there is only an atomic proposition for the outcome of the FI2 flow meter test (testFl2Pass for pass, testFl2Fail for fail), and since this is part of 'all self-test steps passed successfully' (selfTestPass), the 'self-test procedure confirms the FI2 flow meter direction and calibration' is best translated as: 'If the self-test as a whole passes (selfTestPass), then the specific test for the FI2 flow meter must also have passed (testFl2Pass)'. This checks that the self-test's successful completion always means the FI2 test is also passed. The proper template for "if A then B always" is template 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), with bool_exp1 = selfTestPass and bool_exp3 = testFl2Pass. There are no other propositions needed; N_DURATION is not used.

The natural language states that the self-test procedure must confirm that the FI2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit is connected in the right direction and is calibrated. Based on the available atomic propositions, the most relevant signals are those corresponding to tests of the FI2 flow meter: 'testFl2Pass' (FI2 flow meter test pass) and 'testFl2Fail' (FI2 flow meter test failure). The objective is to require that the system doesn't pass overall self-test ('selfTestPass') unless 'testFl2Pass' (i.e., correct connection and calibration of the FI2 flow meter) occurs. Therefore, we require that whenever the self-test passes, it implies the FI2 flow meter test also passes. This is captured by the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' where bool_exp1 = selfTestPass (self-test passes), and bool_exp3 = testFl2Pass (FI2 flow meter confirmed). We do not have direct signals for 'direction' or 'calibrated', but the test pass is the best available proxy for both conditions being met. Thus, this LTL formula ensures that the system shall never consider the self-test as having passed unless the FI2 flow meter is confirmed good by its functional test.

The natural language statement requires that during the self-test procedure, the FI2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit be checked for correct direction and calibration. In the list of atomic propositions, the most relevant indicator is 'testFl2Pass' (FI2 flow meter test pass) and 'selfTestPass' (self-test process passed all steps). The requirement says that the self-test must confirm that the FI2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit is connected in the right direction and calibrated. The input requires that the self-test confirms the proper connection and calibration of the FI2 flow meter in the patient breathing circuit. There is an atomic proposition 'testFl2Pass', which directly maps to the successful Fl2 flow meter test, which encompasses correct connection and calibration. The correct mapping is to require (with a safety/liveness property) that the self-test can only pass when the Fl2 flow meter test also succeeds. The template 'G (selfTestPass -> testFl2Pass)' expresses that globally (at all times), whenever the self-test indicates all steps passed ('selfTestPass'), it is implied that the Fl2 flow meter test must also have passed ('testFl2Pass'), ensuring the requirement that the self-test includes confirmation of both correct orientation and calibration for the FI2 flow meter.
